---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```
```{r}
data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vTOZXdRndVIMRFYRqo8GkBCNW7yI7HZZ6r9SLRqIbG6Vpckg6bPxORzv-T2z-U8qNPkOcWny-kBTqye/pub?gid=751192119&single=true&output=csv")

latest_data <- data |>
  rename(
    timestamp = "Timestamp",
    content_type = "Q1: What type of content appears on your feed?",
    scroll_time = "Q2: On average, in minutes, how often do you scroll reels a day?",
    change_feed = "Q3: Is there anything you wish you could change about your feed?"
  ) |>
  mutate(scroll_category = case_when(
    scroll_time < 10 ~ "Less than 10 mins",
    scroll_time < 30 ~ "10 to 30 mins",
    scroll_time < 60 ~ "30 to 60 mins",
    scroll_time >= 60 ~ "More than 60 mins"
  ))
```
```{css}
body {
  font-family: sans-serif;
  max-width: 800px;
  margin: auto;
  padding: 20px;
}
```

## What's going on on people's Instagram feeds?

This report studies how peoples interactions with their instagram feeds, and how they feel about the content they consume, with relation to what that content is and how long they spend roughly scrolling on the app.

A total of `r nrow(latest_data)` responses were collected. The average time spent scrolling reels was `r round(mean(latest_data$scroll_time), 1)` minutes.
```{r}
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = content_type, fill = content_type)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "What type of content appears on respondents' feeds?",
    x = "Content type",
    y = "Number of respondents"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```
```{r}
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = scroll_category)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "How long do respondents spend scrolling reels?",
    x = "Time spent scrolling",
    y = "Number of respondents"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
